name: Cannubi
description: Most famous and prestigious MGA in Barolo. Elegant, perfumed wines with great finesse and aging potential.
historicalNotes: First documented Barolo vineyard (1700s). Produced first modern Barolo in 1850s. Considered the 'Romanée-Conti of Barolo.'

name: Brunate
description: Straddles La Morra and Barolo communes. Powerful yet elegant wines with complex aromatics.
historicalNotes: Name possibly from 'brunatu' (scorched) due to sunny exposure. Conterno, Rinaldi, Marcarini make stellar examples.

name: Cerequio
description: High-elevation MGA producing refined, perfumed wines. Considered one of the finest in La Morra.
historicalNotes: Roberto Voerzio makes iconic examples. Michele Chiarlo also owns significant holdings.

name: Monprivato
description: Monopole of Giuseppe Mascarello. Structured, classic Barolo with exceptional aging potential.
historicalNotes: Single-owner cru since 1800s. Produces some of the longest-lived wines in Piedmont.

name: Bussia
description: Large, prestigious MGA in Monforte. Powerful, structured wines. Contains several sub-MGAs.
historicalNotes: Prunotto, Aldo Conterno, Oddero make benchmark examples. One of the largest MGAs.

name: Vigna Rionda
description: Small, highly prestigious MGA. Powerful, complex wines with great aging potential. Often considered best in Serralunga.
historicalNotes: Massolino and Oddero own key parcels. Name means 'round vineyard' in local dialect.

name: Lazzarito
description: Serralunga MGA producing powerful, long-lived wines with classic structure.

name: Rocche di Castiglione
description: Steep, rocky slopes producing intense, mineral-driven wines. One of Castiglione Falletto's finest.
historicalNotes: Bruno Giacosa, Vietti, Brovia make legendary examples. 'Rocche' means rocky outcrops.

name: Villero
description: Castiglione Falletto MGA known for powerful, structured wines with great elegance.
historicalNotes: Brovia, Vietti, Cordero make outstanding examples.

name: Francia
description: High-altitude Serralunga MGA. Powerful, concentrated wines needing long aging.
historicalNotes: Giacomo Conterno's legendary single-vineyard bottling. Benchmark for traditional Barolo.

name: Le Rocche dell'Annunziata
description: La Morra's steepest and highest vineyard. Elegant, perfumed wines with finesse.
historicalNotes: Roberto Voerzio, Paolo Scavino, Renato Ratti make stellar examples.

name: Ginestra
description: Large Monforte MGA with significant variation. Contains several prestigious sub-zones.
historicalNotes: Conterno Fantino, Elio Grasso, Clerico make benchmark wines. Contains famous Vigna Casa Maté.

name: Ravera
description: Straddles Novello, Barolo, and Monforte. Elegant wines with good structure.
historicalNotes: Vietti, G.D. Vajra make excellent examples from different communes.

name: Liste
description: Barolo commune MGA producing structured, classic wines.

name: Gavarini
description: Small Serralunga MGA. Part of the larger Vigna Rionda area.
historicalNotes: Elio Grasso makes acclaimed single-vineyard bottling.

name: Bricco Boschis
description: High-elevation Castiglione Falletto MGA. Elegant, perfumed wines.
historicalNotes: Cavallotto owns the majority as monopole (Bricco Boschis Vigna San Giuseppe).

name: Castellero
description: Castiglione Falletto MGA producing structured, mineral wines.

name: Perno
description: Monforte MGA producing elegant, aromatic wines.
historicalNotes: Conterno Fantino makes benchmark examples from Vigna del Gris.

name: Prapò
description: Serralunga MGA known for powerful, structured wines.
historicalNotes: Ceretto (Bricco Rocche estate) makes acclaimed examples.
